stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Both alkaline and zinc-carbon batteries are widely used disposable batteries that convert chemical energy into electricity.
  • Alkaline batteries use potassium hydroxide as their electrolyte.
  • Zinc-carbon batteries use ammonium chloride as their electrolyte.
  • To power high-drain devices like digital cameras, alkaline batteries deliver energy efficiently over a long period.
  • Zinc-carbon batteries are best suited to low-drain devices like remote controls and clocks.
stem: The student wants to emphasize a difference between alkaline batteries and zinc-carbon batteries. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Alkaline batteries and zinc-carbon batteries are both widely used disposable batteries that convert chemical energy into electricity.
Alkaline batteries are best suited to high-drain devices like digital cameras, whereas zinc-carbon batteries are best suited to low-drain devices like remote controls.
To power devices, bakers and engineers rely on disposable batteries, including alkaline and zinc-carbon types.
Potassium hydroxide is the electrolyte in alkaline batteries, and ammonium chloride is a common chemical compound.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The sentence emphasizes a difference between alkaline and zinc-carbon batteries, noting that alkaline batteries are best suited to high-drain devices while zinc-carbon batteries are best suited to low-drain devices.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence emphasizes a similarity between the two battery types; it doesn’t emphasize a difference. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence describes general battery use; it doesn’t emphasize a difference between alkaline and zinc-carbon batteries. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence mentions only the electrolyte of alkaline batteries directly; it doesn’t contrast it with the electrolyte of zinc-carbon batteries.
